description:
## Component: hx-badge (UPGRADE)
**Tier 1 — Universal Foundation | Industry Consensus: 5/6 libraries**

Upgrade existing scaffold. Small status or count indicator for labels, notifications, and categorization.

### ADR-001 Classification: Property-Driven (Atomic)
Properties control variant and visual style. Default slot accepts text content.

### API Design
**Properties:** `variant` (primary|secondary|success|warning|danger|neutral|info), `hx-size` (sm|md|lg), `pill` (boolean — rounded), `pulse` (boolean — attention animation), `removable` (boolean — shows X)
**Slots:** Default (badge text), `prefix` (icon)
**CSS Parts:** `badge`, `remove-button`
**Events:** `hx-remove` (when removable badge dismissed)

### Drupal Integration
```twig
{# Patient status badge #}
<hx-badge variant="{% if status == 'active' %}success{% elseif status == 'critical' %}danger{% else %}neutral{% endif %}">
  {{ status|capitalize }}
</hx-badge>

{# Notification count #}
<hx-badge variant="danger" pill>{{ unread_count }}</hx-badge>

{# Taxonomy term badges #}
{% for term in node.field_tags %}
  <hx-badge variant="secondary">{{ term.entity.label }}</hx-badge>
{% endfor %}
```

### Healthcare: Patient status, priority indicators, department tags, allergy flags, notification counts

### Acceptance Criteria
1. All semantic variants with design token colors
2. Pill mode for count badges
3. Pulse animation for attention-critical badges
4. Removable mode with X button and hx-remove event
5. Works inline with text and in hx-card footer slots
title: T1-19: hx-badge — Status/Count Indicator

## Component: hx-badge (UPGRADE)
**Tier 1 — Universal Foundation | Industry Consensus: 5/6 libraries**

Upgrade existing scaffold. Small status or count indicator for labels, notifications, and categorization.

### ADR-001 Classification: Property-Driven (Atomic)
Properties control variant and visual style. Default slot accepts text content.

### API Design
**Properties:** `variant` (primary|secondary|success|warning|danger|neutral|info), `hx-size` (sm|md|lg), `pill` (boolean — rounded), `pulse` (boolean — attention animation), `removable` (boolean — shows X)
**Slots:** Default (badge text), `prefix` (icon)
**CSS Parts:** `badge`, `remove-button`
**Events:** `hx-remove` (when removable badge dismissed)

### Drupal Integration
```twig
{# Patient status badge #}
<hx-badge variant="{% if status == 'active' %}success{% elseif status == 'critical' %}danger{% else %}neutral{% endif %}">
  {{ status|capitalize }}
</hx-badge>

{# Notification count #}
<hx-badge variant="danger" pill>{{ unread_count }}</hx-badge>

{# Taxonomy term badges #}
{% for term in node.field_tags %}
  <hx-badge variant="secondary">{{ term.entity.label }}</hx-badge>
{% endfor %}
```

### Healthcare: Patient status, priority indicators, department tags, allergy flags, notification counts

### Acceptance Criteria
1. All semantic variants with design token colors
2. Pill mode for count badges
3. Pulse animation for attention-critical badges
4. Removable mode with X button and hx-remove event
5. Works inline with text and in hx-card footer slots

lastReviewFeedback:
coderabbitai: **Actionable comments posted: 4**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-badge/hx-badge.styles.ts`:
- Line 12: Replace hardcoded spacing and focus metrics in hx-badge.styles.ts:
remove the literal fallback in the gap declaration (change uses of gap:
var(--hx-space-1, 0.25rem); to rely on the --hx-space-* token only) and replace
any fixed focus geometry values on the badge focus/active rules (the blocks
around lines ~140-145 and ~153-154) with the appropriate design tokens (e.g.,
--hx-focus-ring-width, --hx-focus-offset, --hx-radius or equivalent spacing
tokens) so all spacing, focus ring thickness, offsets, and radii reference CSS
custom properties rather than hardcoded numbers; update the selectors where
these styles are applied (the hx-badge host or .hx-badge focus/active rules) to
use the token names used across the codebase.
- Around line 52-56: The variant classes (e.g., .badge--secondary in
hx-badge.styles.ts and the other two variant blocks) define component vars but
don't read the new variant-level public tokens; update each variant block to
derive the component-level tokens from variant-specific tokens with semantic
fallbacks—e.g., set --hx-badge-bg: var(--hx-badge-secondary-bg,
var(--hx-color-neutral-100, `#f3f4f6`)), --hx-badge-color:
var(--hx-badge-secondary-color, var(--hx-color-neutral-700, `#374151`)), and
--hx-badge-pulse-color: var(--hx-badge-secondary-pulse-color,
var(--hx-color-neutral-100, `#f3f4f6`)); apply the same pattern for the other two
variant classes using their respective names (replace "secondary" with the
variant identifier) so component code (which consumes --hx-badge-bg /
--hx-badge-color / --hx-badge-pulse-color) respects the new three-tier token
cascade.

In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-badge/hx-badge.ts`:
- Around line 48-56: The p

threadFeedback.agentReasoning: The bot flags hardcoded `2px` and `1px` focus ring metrics in the remove button's `:focus-visible` rule, which violates the "no hardcoded values" policy. The spacing token fallbacks (like `0.25rem`) are defensive fallbacks and should be kept, but the focus ring width and offset should reference design tokens. I'll use token fallbacks so behavior is unchanged if tokens aren't loaded.

This is a real bug: the JSDoc documents variant-specific public CSS custom properties (`--hx-badge-secondary-bg`, `--hx-badge-secondary-color`, `--hx-badge-danger-bg`, etc.) that consumers can override, but the CSS variant classes bypass them entirely — setting `--hx-badge-bg` directly from the semantic token. This breaks the three-tier cascade architecture. A consumer who sets `--hx-badge-secondary-bg: hotpink` would see no effect.

The intentional retention of `error` alongside `danger` was a backward-compatibility decision documented in the implementation. Making `error` internal with a console deprecation warning changes observable behavior (noisy warnings for existing users), requires updating all tests and stories, and is out of scope for this PR. This trivial nitpick doesn't justify the change surface or the behavioral regression for existing `variant="error"` consumers.
